ontology.foodon_gap_note: FoodOn has a generic dumpling class (FOODON_03315763); no exact class for steamed pork-and-vegetable jiao exists. Closest parent match used.
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 code A0FKP covers steamed dumplings as a broader category; no specific pork-and-vegetable steamed jiao code exists in the published FoodEx2 catalogue. Closest parent match used.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No SNOMED CT concept for steamed pork-and-vegetable dumplings identified in the published SNOMED CT food hierarchy.

serving.natural_language_portion: A typical serving of steamed vegetable and pork dumplings is 4 pieces, weighing approximately 120 g, as commonly served at hawker centres and dim sum restaurants in Singapore.

nutrient_source.title: 菜肉蒸饺 Steamed Vegetable & Pork Dumplings — Singapore Health Promotion Board Foodo Database
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: Nutrient values sourced directly from the HPB Singapore Foodo database entry for 菜肉蒸饺 Steamed Vegetable & Pork Dumplings (cr_id G00010043). All per-100g values are taken as reported. Saturated fat and dietary fibre are not reported in the HPB entry and are set to -1 with documentation in notes.

gi_source.title: International tables of glycemic index and glycemic load values 2008 — steamed wheat-flour dumplings analogous estimate
gi_source.organization: University of Sydney GI Database / Atkinson FS, Foster-Powell K, Brand-Miller JC. Diabetes Care 2008;31(12):2281-2283.
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement for steamed pork-and-vegetable jiao exists in the University of Sydney GI Database. GI of 48 is estimated from steamed wheat-flour dumplings (jiaozi) as the closest analogous food reported in Atkinson et al. 2008 (Diabetes Care). Glycemic load calculated as (GI × carbohydrate_g per serving) / 100 = (48 × 9.6) / 100 ≈ 4.6 for a 120 g serving (9.6 g carbohydrate).

hypertension.key_factor: Very low sodium at 46 mg per 100 g
hypertension.guidance: At 46 mg sodium per 100 g, steamed vegetable and pork dumplings are well within safe limits for hypertensive individuals; however, patients should avoid high-sodium dipping sauces such as soy sauce or chilli sauce, which can substantially increase total sodium intake per meal.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Low carbohydrate (8 g/100 g), negligible sugar (0.2 g/100 g), and estimated low GI of 48
type2_diabetes.guidance: With a low estimated GI of 48, only 8 g carbohydrate per 100 g, and 8.7 g protein per 100 g to moderate postprandial glucose response, steamed vegetable and pork dumplings are suitable for individuals with type 2 diabetes when consumed in a portion of 4–5 pieces and paired with non-starchy vegetables; sweet dipping sauces should be avoided.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Low total fat (3.78 g/100 g), low sodium (46 mg/100 g), and moderate cholesterol (28 mg/100 g)
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: The low total fat content, very low sodium, and steaming preparation method make this dish a heart-friendly choice; individuals with cardiovascular disease should limit portion size to manage cholesterol intake and avoid high-fat or high-sodium condiments.

rheumatoid_arthritis.key_factor: Pork filling contributes arachidonic acid (omega-6), which may amplify inflammatory pathways
rheumatoid_arthritis.guidance: Pork is a source of arachidonic acid that can amplify TNF-alpha and IL-6 inflammatory pathways; individuals with rheumatoid arthritis should consider substituting pork with chicken or tofu-vegetable filling and avoiding high-sodium dipping sauces.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Steamed Vegetable and Pork Dumplings (菜肉蒸饺, Cài Ròu Zhēng Jiǎo) are a classic Chinese dim sum item widely enjoyed in Singapore's hawker centres, coffee shops, and dim sum restaurants. Each dumpling consists of a thin, hand-rolled wheat flour wrapper encasing a fragrant filling of minced pork, bok choy (Chinese white cabbage), and chives, seasoned with soy sauce, sesame oil, and white pepper. The dumplings are cooked entirely by steaming, which preserves moisture and produces a tender, slightly chewy wrapper with a juicy, savoury filling. The texture is soft and delicate, with the vegetable filling providing a mild sweetness that balances the umami of the pork. Nutritionally, per 100 g the dish provides 101 kcal, 8.71 g protein, 3.78 g fat, and 8.03 g carbohydrate, with very low sodium at 46 mg and negligible sugar at 0.21 g. The estimated glycemic index is 48 (low), making it suitable for blood glucose management. The low fat and sodium content, combined with the steaming method, make this a relatively heart-friendly and diabetes-friendly option among Chinese dim sum items. It is commonly served as a light meal or snack at lunch or dinner, typically accompanied by a small dish of soy-vinegar dipping sauce. Clinically, the low sodium and low GI profile are favourable for hypertension and type 2 diabetes management, though dipping sauces should be used sparingly.

confidence_reason: Nutrient values sourced directly from the HPB Singapore Foodo database (cr_id G00010043), which is the primary approved source for Singapore dishes. The exact dish entry was matched. GI is an analogous estimate from the University of Sydney GI Database via Atkinson et al. 2008, which is the approved GI source; this reduces certainty for the GI component only, but the overall record confidence remains high given the exact HPB match for all macros and micronutrients.
notes: 1. saturated_fat_g, monounsaturated_fat_g, polyunsaturated_fat_g, trans_fat_g, dietary_fibre_g, vitamin_c_mg, vitamin_a_mcg_rae, vitamin_b1_thiamine_mg, vitamin_b2_riboflavin_mg, vitamin_b3_niacin_mg, vitamin_b6_mg, vitamin_b12_mcg, folate_mcg_dfe, vitamin_d_mcg, vitamin_e_mg, phosphorus_mg, zinc_mg, selenium_mcg, water_g set to -1: these fields are not reported in the HPB Singapore Foodo database entry for this dish (G00010043) as of the accessed date. No secondary approved source (USDA FoodData Central) contains an exact match for this specific Singapore HPB dish formulation. 2. GI of 48 is an analogous estimate based on steamed wheat-flour dumplings (jiaozi) from Atkinson FS et al., Diabetes Care 2008;31(12):2281-2283, via the University of Sydney GI Database. No direct GI measurement for this specific dish exists in the approved GI sources. 3. Glycemic load per serving calculated as (48 × 9.636 g carbohydrate in 120 g serving) / 100 = 4.6. 4. Halal status is not confirmed; pork is a primary ingredient and this dish is not halal. is_halal set to false. 5. No zero-value core macros are present; zero-macro justification rule (g4) is not triggered.
